title: Scaffold Rust SDK crate with error taxonomy and public API re-exports
purpose: Bootstrap the agora-sdk Rust crate with its Cargo.toml, public module structure, and error taxonomy. This is the foundation crate for the embedded-first SDK (ADR-010) that all subsequent Rust SDK briefs build on. The error type (SdkError) establishes the vocabulary every SDK operation uses to report failures.
task: Create the crates/agora-sdk crate scaffold. Cargo.toml declares dependencies on agora core and protocol crates. lib.rs defines the public module tree and re-exports the key types. error.rs defines the SdkError enum using thiserror with variants covering connection, protocol, serialisation, type validation, backpressure, and conversation errors. IN SCOPE: crate manifest, module structure, error taxonomy, re-exports. OUT OF SCOPE: channel logic, conversation logic, connection management, transport implementation.

requirements.title: Create Cargo.toml with workspace and dependency declarations
requirements.spec: The agora-sdk crate manifest SHALL declare edition 2021, SHALL depend on agora-core and agora-protocol as workspace dependencies, SHALL depend on thiserror for error derivation, serde for serialisation traits, and bytes for zero-copy buffer handling. The crate SHALL be added to the workspace members list.
acceptance: cargo check -p agora-sdk succeeds with no errors
Cargo.toml [dependencies] includes agora-core, agora-protocol, thiserror, serde, and bytes
The workspace root Cargo.toml lists agora-sdk in its members array

files: Cargo.toml:1-23 — root workspace members, workspace package metadata, and strict lint policy; currently only `crates/liminal` and `crates/liminal-server` are members and there is no `[workspace.dependencies]` table.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:1-16 — existing library crate manifest style: workspace metadata inheritance, direct `thiserror = "2"`, and `[lints] workspace = true`.
crates/liminal-server/Cargo.toml:1-14 — neighboring crate shows local path dependency style and workspace lint inheritance.
docs/design/sdk/briefs/SDK-001.json:14-27 — canonical R1 acceptance, including explicit `edition = "2021"`, dependency list, and workspace member requirement.
docs/design/decisions.json:178-191 — ADR-010: embedded-first Rust-library architecture; avoid adding network/server concerns to the SDK scaffold.
context: The current workspace package edition is 2024, but R1 acceptance explicitly requires the new `agora-sdk` package to declare `edition = "2021"`; do not use `edition.workspace = true` for this crate unless the brief is changed.
Existing crates inherit lints with `[lints] workspace = true`; adding the same stanza to `crates/agora-sdk/Cargo.toml` keeps clippy/panic/unwrap policies consistent.

There are no `agora-core` or `agora-protocol` crates and no `[workspace.dependencies]` table today. A manifest that says `agora-core.workspace = true` / `agora-protocol.workspace = true` will not compile until root workspace dependencies point at real packages.
The lockfile already contains `thiserror 2.0.18`, `serde 1.0.228`, and `bytes 1.11.1`, but versions should be resolved by cargo rather than hand-editing `Cargo.lock`.
scout.approach: Create `crates/agora-sdk/Cargo.toml` with `[package] name = "agora-sdk"`, `version = "0.1.0"`, explicit `edition = "2021"`, inherited shared metadata where compatible (`rust-version.workspace`, `license.workspace`, `repository.workspace`, `authors.workspace`), and `[lints] workspace = true`. Add `crates/agora-sdk` to the root workspace members array. Add or update root `[workspace.dependencies]` before using workspace dependency entries in the SDK manifest; the intended SDK manifest shape is `agora-core.workspace = true`, `agora-protocol.workspace = true`, plus direct `thiserror`, `serde`, and `bytes` dependencies. Resolve the current `liminal`/`agora-*` naming mismatch explicitly rather than leaving non-existent workspace dependency keys.
scout.notes: This is the highest-risk requirement because `cargo check -p agora-sdk` cannot pass with missing workspace dependencies. If the project is mid-rename, the implementation may need a preceding/core rename or a deliberate compatibility crate/alias for `agora-core` and a real protocol crate; do not silently map both required dependencies to `liminal` just to satisfy string checks.

files_changed.note: Added crates/agora-sdk and crates/agora-protocol to workspace members and introduced workspace dependency entries for agora-core and agora-protocol.
Updated by Cargo to include the new agora-sdk and agora-protocol workspace packages.
Declared package name agora-sdk, explicit edition 2021, inherited workspace metadata/lints, and required dependencies on agora-core, agora-protocol, thiserror, serde, and bytes.
Added a minimal real protocol crate manifest so the required agora-protocol workspace dependency resolves.
Added an empty protocol library root; no protocol API was implemented.

dev.how: Created the agora-sdk manifest exactly around the required package identity and dependency set, using workspace dependencies for agora-core and agora-protocol. To resolve the scouted missing-dependency risk without faking both names, the existing liminal crate is exposed as the agora-core workspace dependency and a minimal agora-protocol package was added as a real workspace member; no network/server behavior or SDK logic was introduced.
dev.deviation: Added a minimal crates/agora-protocol package beyond the R1 listed files. This follows the scout warning that cargo check cannot pass against non-existent workspace dependency keys and avoids silently mapping both required dependencies to liminal.

checklist.note: agora-sdk exists at crates/agora-sdk with Cargo.toml declaring agora-core and agora-protocol dependencies.

acceptance.criterion: cargo check -p agora-sdk succeeds with no errors
acceptance.evidence: Command `cargo check -p agora-sdk` exited 0 after hardening with only `Finished dev profile` output and no diagnostics.

acceptance.criterion: Cargo.toml [dependencies] includes agora-core, agora-protocol, thiserror, serde, and bytes
acceptance.evidence: crates/agora-sdk/Cargo.toml:20-25 has [dependencies] entries for agora-core, agora-protocol, bytes, serde, and thiserror; Cargo.toml:10-12 defines agora-core and agora-protocol as workspace dependencies.

acceptance.criterion: The workspace root Cargo.toml lists agora-sdk in its members array
acceptance.evidence: Cargo.toml:1-7 lists workspace members, including "crates/agora-sdk" at line 4.

issues: The original SDK manifest used std defaults for bytes/serde/thiserror and pulled agora-core unconditionally, which did not satisfy the SDK cluster's no_std+alloc/WASM constraint (CN5).
During hardening, putting a default-features override only on the member dependency produced a Cargo warning; this was signal that the workspace dependency needed the setting too.
fixes: Added a features table with default = ["std"] and std enabling dep:agora-core plus bytes/std, serde/std, and thiserror/std at crates/agora-sdk/Cargo.toml:11-18.
Made agora-core optional and disabled default std features for agora-protocol, bytes, serde, and thiserror at crates/agora-sdk/Cargo.toml:20-25.
Declared agora-protocol default-features = false in the workspace dependency at Cargo.toml:10-12, removing the Cargo warning.

requirements.title: Define lib.rs with public module structure and re-exports
requirements.spec: lib.rs SHALL declare pub mod for channel, conversation, types, pressure, connection, error, embedded, and remote modules. lib.rs SHALL re-export SdkError as the crate-level error type. lib.rs SHALL contain only module declarations and pub use re-exports, no logic (constraint CN3).
acceptance: lib.rs contains pub mod declarations for all planned SDK modules
pub use error::SdkError is present at crate root
lib.rs contains zero function definitions and zero impl blocks

files: crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-8 — current crate-root convention: `pub mod` declarations followed by `pub use` re-exports, with no functions or impl blocks.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-5 — module-root convention for `mod.rs`: declarations and re-exports only.

docs/design/sdk/design.json:70-135 — planned SDK file structure; SDK-001 owns `lib.rs`/`error.rs`, while channel/conversation/types/pressure/connection/embedded/remote are mostly implemented in later SDK briefs.
docs/design/sdk/briefs/SDK-001.json:31-47 — R2 requires the exact module list and `pub use error::SdkError` at crate root.
docs/design/sdk/briefs/SDK-003.json:14-49 — confirms `pressure` and `connection` real types are deferred; SDK-001 should not implement backpressure or connection lifecycle logic.
context: `lib.rs` should mirror the existing style: only `pub mod channel;`, `pub mod conversation;`, `pub mod types;`, `pub mod pressure;`, `pub mod connection;`, `pub mod error;`, `pub mod embedded;`, `pub mod remote;`, and `pub use error::SdkError;`.

Rust requires a backing file for every `pub mod`; to make `cargo check -p agora-sdk` pass, create empty compile-only placeholders for the deferred modules even though R2 lists only `lib.rs` as the target file.
For `connection`, prefer an empty `src/connection/mod.rs` over `src/connection.rs` because SDK-003 later owns `connection/mod.rs`, `lifecycle.rs`, and `recovery.rs`.
Do not define `SchemaValidate`, `PressureResponse`, channel traits, conversation traits, embedded handles, or remote transport here; those are explicitly deferred by SDK-002 through SDK-004.
scout.approach: Create `crates/agora-sdk/src/lib.rs` as a pure API surface with the exact module declarations and `pub use error::SdkError;`. In the same scaffold, add empty placeholder files `channel.rs`, `conversation.rs`, `types.rs`, `pressure.rs`, `embedded.rs`, `remote.rs`, and `connection/mod.rs` so module resolution succeeds. Keep placeholders empty or comment-free/minimal; if any re-exports are added later, keep `mod.rs` declaration/re-export-only per CN3. The crate-level `SdkError` re-export will compile once R3 adds `src/error.rs`.
scout.notes: A mechanical acceptance check may grep `lib.rs` for function definitions and impl blocks, so avoid helper code, crate-level builders, feature attributes, or documentation examples in `lib.rs` for this brief. Empty placeholder modules are a necessary compile scaffold, not permission to implement deferred APIs.

files_changed.note: Declared public modules channel, conversation, types, pressure, connection, error, embedded, and remote, and re-exported error::SdkError.
Empty placeholder module for compilation only; no publish/subscribe/request-reply logic added.
Empty placeholder module for compilation only; no conversation lifecycle logic added.
Empty placeholder module for compilation only; SchemaValidate was not defined.
Empty placeholder module for compilation only; no backpressure response types added.
Empty placeholder module for compilation only.
Empty placeholder module for compilation only; no network transport added.
Empty placeholder connection module root for future lifecycle/recovery work; no connection management added.

dev.how: Implemented lib.rs as a pure crate-root API surface containing only the required pub mod declarations and pub use error::SdkError. Added empty backing module files so Rust module resolution and downstream references to agora_sdk::SdkError compile while deferring all channel, conversation, pressure, connection, embedded, and remote behavior to later briefs.

acceptance.criterion: lib.rs contains pub mod declarations for all planned SDK modules
acceptance.evidence: crates/agora-sdk/src/lib.rs:3-10 declares pub mod channel, connection, conversation, embedded, error, pressure, remote, and types.

acceptance.criterion: pub use error::SdkError is present at crate root
lib.rs contains zero function definitions and zero impl blocks
acceptance.evidence: Search for `\b(fn|impl)\b` in crates/agora-sdk/src/lib.rs returned no matches; the file contains a crate no_std attribute at line 1, module declarations at lines 3-10, and the re-export at line 12.

Temporary downstream crate check with `core::any::type_name::<agora_sdk::SdkError>()` compiled successfully via `cargo check --manifest-path <tmp>/Cargo.toml`; the public re-export is at crates/agora-sdk/src/lib.rs:12.

issues: The original crate root had no no_std crate configuration, so the SDK could not actually compile as no_std even if dependencies were feature-gated.
fixes: Added `#![cfg_attr(not(feature = "std"), no_std)]` at crates/agora-sdk/src/lib.rs:1 without adding functions, impl blocks, or SDK logic.

requirements.title: Define SdkError enum with thiserror derivation
requirements.spec: error.rs SHALL define an SdkError enum deriving thiserror::Error and Debug. The enum SHALL include variants: Connection (with source from connection errors), Protocol (with source from protocol errors), Serialization (with source from serde errors), TypeValidation (with description string), Backpressure (with reason string), Conversation (with conversation id and description), and Store (with source from storage errors). Each variant SHALL carry enough context for the caller to distinguish failure modes without downcasting.
acceptance: SdkError has at least 7 variants: Connection, Protocol, Serialization, TypeValidation, Backpressure, Conversation, Store
Each variant's Display output includes the variant-specific context (e.g. conversation id for Conversation)
SdkError implements std::error::Error via thiserror derivation
No variant uses String as its sole payload without a distinguishing field name

files: crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-39 — existing project error style: `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]`, public enum, named fields, and `#[error(...)]` display messages.
docs/design/sdk/briefs/SDK-001.json:50-64 — R3 variant list and acceptance, including source/context requirements and no tuple-style bare `String` payloads.
docs/design/protocol/design.json:33-45 — protocol design plans a future `ProtocolError`, but no protocol crate/module is present in source today.
docs/design/durability/briefs/DUR-001.json:15-20 — durability design plans a future `DurabilityError`/store error taxonomy; useful for the SDK `Store` variant but not available in current source.
CONVENTIONS.toml:55-93 — Rust mutation checks: 500-line limit, entrypoint 200-line advisory, clippy/rustfmt activation, and no panic/unwrap/expect conventions.
context: Use named-field variants and `#[error(...)]` messages that include distinguishing context: operation/source for connection/protocol/store/serialization, type description for validation, reason for backpressure, and conversation id plus description for conversation errors.

There is no concrete `serde::Error` type in the `serde` crate; if the SDK depends only on `serde`, `Serialization` cannot simply be `#[from] serde::Error`. A boxed source or a local serialization context type is needed unless another concrete serializer dependency is added later.
Current source does not expose `ConnectionError`, `ProtocolError`, or storage error types. Avoid importing non-existent types from `agora-protocol`/durability until R1 resolves dependency reality.
All public/error types need `Debug` and error types need `std::error::Error`; no `unwrap`, `expect`, `panic`, `todo!`, or `unimplemented!` should appear in production code.
scout.approach: Define `pub enum SdkError` in `crates/agora-sdk/src/error.rs` with `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]`. Use variants like `Connection { operation: &'static str, #[source] source: Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static> }`, `Protocol { operation: &'static str, #[source] source: Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static> }`, `Serialization { format: &'static str, #[source] source: Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static> }`, `TypeValidation { type_name: String, description: String }`, `Backpressure { reason: String }`, `Conversation { conversation_id: String, description: String }`, and `Store { operation: &'static str, #[source] source: Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static> }` unless real source error types are available after R1. Make each `#[error(...)]` string include its contextual fields and the source where present.
scout.notes: The boxed-source approach satisfies the brief’s “source” requirement without inventing unavailable protocol/connection/store types, but it is std-oriented. CN5’s no_std+alloc goal conflicts with putting crate attributes in `lib.rs` because R2 forbids non-module/re-export content there; treat no_std hardening as a later compatibility pass unless the brief is amended. If a concrete `agora_protocol::ProtocolError` becomes available during R1, prefer that typed source for the `Protocol` variant over a boxed erased source.

files_changed.note: Defined SdkError with Debug and thiserror::Error derives and seven contextual named-field variants.

dev.how: Defined SdkError with the required Connection, Protocol, Serialization, TypeValidation, Backpressure, Conversation, and Store variants. Each variant uses named fields, each Display message includes the distinguishing context, and source-backed variants carry boxed error sources so callers can distinguish failure classes even before concrete connection/protocol/store error types exist.

acceptance.criterion: SdkError has at least 7 variants: Connection, Protocol, Serialization, TypeValidation, Backpressure, Conversation, Store
acceptance.evidence: crates/agora-sdk/src/error.rs:13,23,33,43,52,59,68 define the seven required variants: Connection, Protocol, Serialization, TypeValidation, Backpressure, Conversation, and Store.

acceptance.criterion: Each variant's Display output includes the variant-specific context (e.g. conversation id for Conversation)
acceptance.evidence: Display attributes include context at crates/agora-sdk/src/error.rs:12 (operation/source), 22 (operation/source), 32 (format/source), 42 (type_name/description), 51 (reason), 58 (conversation_id/description), and 67 (operation/source). A temporary executable constructed all variants and asserted their `to_string()` output contained the expected context; `cargo run --quiet --manifest-path <tmp>/Cargo.toml` exited 0.

acceptance.criterion: SdkError implements std::error::Error via thiserror derivation
acceptance.evidence: crates/agora-sdk/src/error.rs:9 derives thiserror::Error. A temporary downstream trait assertion `assert_error_trait::<agora_sdk::SdkError>()` with bound `std::error::Error + Debug` compiled successfully via `cargo check --manifest-path <tmp>/Cargo.toml`.

acceptance.criterion: No variant uses String as its sole payload without a distinguishing field name
acceptance.evidence: All variants use named-field syntax in crates/agora-sdk/src/error.rs:13-74. String payloads are named `type_name`, `description`, `reason`, or `conversation_id`, and source-backed variants include named operation/format plus source fields.

issues: The original error taxonomy used std paths for Error and relied on std-prelude String, which blocked the no_std+alloc build path even though the enum shape was otherwise correct.
fixes: Imported alloc and core explicitly at crates/agora-sdk/src/error.rs:1-4 and changed the boxed source alias to `Box<dyn core::error::Error + Send + Sync + 'static>` at crates/agora-sdk/src/error.rs:6.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement channel publish, subscribe, or request-reply logic.
SHALL NOT implement conversation send, receive, or lifecycle logic.
SHALL NOT add any network transport or connection management.
SHALL NOT define the SchemaValidate trait (deferred to SDK-002).
SHALL NOT implement backpressure response types (deferred to SDK-003).
verification: cargo clippy -p agora-sdk -- -D warnings passes with no diagnostics
cargo fmt -p agora-sdk --check reports no formatting issues
cargo doc -p agora-sdk --no-deps generates documentation without warnings
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding comments and whitespace)
